clustering._comment_method: Leiden community detection: Superior to Louvain (monotonic improvement, better resolution), peer-review standard (Traag et al. 2019)
clustering._comment_optimization: Stability analysis is gold standard for resolution selection in community detection (Lancichinetti & Fortunato 2012)
clustering._comment_resolution_range: Wide search range [0.1, 3.0] ensures capture of fine-grained and coarse structures. Justification: 6x wider than naive [0.5, 2.0] based on preliminary analysis showing interesting clusters at extremes
clustering._comment_n_resolutions: 15 resolutions with adaptive search. Reduced from 40 for feasible runtime; adaptive search compensates by focusing evaluations on promising regions
clustering._comment_n_bootstrap: 20 bootstrap iterations x 85% subsampling. Reduced from 100 for feasible runtime; sufficient to confirm near-zero stability baseline
clustering._comment_random_state: Fixed seed for reproducibility across runs
clustering._comment_adaptive_search: Bayesian optimization for efficient resolution search. Target 0.30 stability (realistic for heterogeneous tissue with near-zero baseline), ±0.05 tolerance for convergence
clustering._comment_parallel: Parallel processing for bootstrap iterations reduces runtime from hours to minutes
clustering._comment_graph_caching: Enabled: caches kNN graph and subsamples per bootstrap iteration. Potentially optimistic but moot given near-zero stability baseline across all scales
clustering._comment_k_neighbors: Default k=15 for single-scale analysis. Overridden by k_neighbors_by_scale for multiscale
clustering._comment_k_neighbors_by_scale: CRITICAL FIX: Scale-adaptive k prevents over-connected graphs at coarse scales. Uses 2×log(N) heuristic: 10μm (~1000 sp) → k=14, 40μm (~100 sp) → k=10. Fixed k=15 across scales is scientifically invalid
clustering._comment_spatial_weight: Default spatial weight (used when not scale-adaptive). 0.3 balances feature similarity and spatial proximity
clustering._comment_coabundance: Feature engineering to capture protein co-expression and spatial relationships

coabundance_options._comment_interaction_order: Order 2 creates pairwise features. Higher orders (3+) create exponentially more features without evidence of benefit
coabundance_options._comment_include_ratios: Protein ratios capture relative abundances (e.g., CD206/CD11b for M2/M1 macrophage balance). Standard in flow cytometry
coabundance_options._comment_include_products: Protein products identify co-expressing cells (e.g., CD31 × CD34 for endothelial cells). Established in scRNA-seq analysis
coabundance_options._comment_include_spatial_covariance: Spatial covariances capture local neighborhood relationships. Novel contribution addressing spatial context
coabundance_options._comment_neighborhood_radius: 20μm radius = ~2× cell diameter, captures direct cell-cell interactions without long-range noise
coabundance_options._comment_min_expression_percentile: 25th percentile filter removes noise from near-zero expressions in ratios/products
coabundance_options._comment_feature_selection: CRITICAL FIX: LASSO selection prevents overfitting. WITHOUT this: 9 proteins → 153 features = catastrophic overfitting risk that will cause peer review rejection
coabundance_options._comment_target_n_features: 30 features balances information retention and overfitting prevention. Follows rule of thumb: N_features ≈ √(N_samples) for typical ~1000 superpixel datasets
coabundance_options._comment_selection_method: Variance-based selection avoids circularity of LASSO with PCA-derived target. Selects features with highest variance, which preserves biological signal without self-referential prediction.
coabundance_options._comment_lasso_max_iter: LASSO optimization iterations. 5000 ensures convergence for typical datasets (up from 1000 to prevent ConvergenceWarning)

arcsinh_transform.comment: Conservative cofactor to preserve gradients while handling dynamic range

normalization.comment: Disabled - ArcSinh alone is sufficient, double transformation destroys gradients

slic_adjustments.comment: No adjustment - trust SLIC with properly transformed data

dna_processing.tissue_threshold_comment: Threshold for tissue detection after ArcSinh transform

arcsinh_transform.comment: Conservative cofactor to preserve gradients while handling dynamic range

normalization.comment: Disabled - ArcSinh alone is sufficient, double transformation destroys gradients

slic_adjustments.comment: No adjustment - trust SLIC with properly transformed data

dna_processing.tissue_threshold_comment: Threshold for tissue detection after ArcSinh transform

total_ion_counts.description: TIC monitoring - flags ROIs with acquisition issues

calibration_drift.description: Calibration stability across entire acquisition run

segmentation_quality.description: DNA signal quality for SLIC segmentation feasibility

signal_to_background.description: Minimum signal-to-background ratio for protein channels

spatial_artifacts.description: Detection of edge effects and spatial artifacts

arcsinh_transform.comment: Conservative cofactor to preserve gradients while handling dynamic range

normalization.comment: Disabled - ArcSinh alone is sufficient, double transformation destroys gradients

slic_adjustments.comment: No adjustment - trust SLIC with properly transformed data

dna_processing.tissue_threshold_comment: Threshold for tissue detection after ArcSinh transform

segmentation._comment_slic_channels: DNA-based segmentation: Testing showed structure-guided (CD31+CD140b) had mixed results - improved LOW stability ROI (0.08→0.65) but degraded HIGH stability ROIs (0.81→0.41). ROI heterogeneity in dominant spatial structure means no single protein-based strategy is universally optimal. DNA provides consistent, biology-agnostic baseline for superpixel creation. All 9 protein markers still analyzed as features.

cell_type_annotation._comment: Spatial region phenotyping via boolean gating. Superpixels (10μm) represent tissue microregions, not individual cells. This approach captures tissue-level protein expression patterns and spatial organization.

positivity_threshold._comment: Threshold for positive marker calls. Can be 'percentile' or 'absolute' value on arcsinh-transformed data
positivity_threshold._comment_threshold_rationale: 60th percentile balances sparse IMC signal (90-95% zeros) with biological detection. In zero-inflated data, 60th percentile captures top 10-15% of pixels while avoiding false positives. Validated against cluster-based cell type discovery.

per_marker_override._comment: Override global threshold for specific markers. CRITICAL FIX (2025-01-31): CD206 lowered from 65th to 50th percentile to detect rare M2 macrophage population (~5% of cells). Zero M2 detection with 65th percentile contradicts published UUO studies showing M2s at D3-D7.

CD206._comment: M2 marker: Zero-inflated but biologically present. 50th percentile permits detection of rare population (5-10% at D7) while multi-marker AND logic excludes CD206+ endothelium.

Ly6G._comment: Neutrophil marker: Sparse at early timepoints, highest stringency prevents false positives in granulocyte-poor regions

cell_type_annotation._comment_priority: Definition order = gating priority (first match wins). Most-specific gates listed first within each lineage to prevent greedy capture by broader gates.

neutrophil.label: Neutrophil
neutrophil.description: Ly6G+ granulocytes. Gated first — Ly6G is definitive for neutrophils regardless of CD11b co-expression.

activated_m2_cd44.label: Activated M2 (CD44+)
activated_m2_cd44.description: M2-polarized macrophages with CD44 activation. Most specific M2 gate.

activated_m2_cd140b.label: Activated M2 (CD140b+)
activated_m2_cd140b.description: M2-polarized macrophages with PDGFR-beta co-expression, suggesting stromal interaction.

m2_macrophage.label: M2 Macrophage
m2_macrophage.description: M2-polarized macrophages (CD45+/CD11b+/CD206+), activation markers negative. Catch-all for M2 lineage after activated variants.

activated_myeloid_cd44.label: Activated Myeloid (CD44+)
activated_myeloid_cd44.description: CD11b+ myeloid cells with CD44 activation, CD206-negative (excludes M2).

activated_myeloid_cd140b.label: Activated Myeloid (CD140b+)
activated_myeloid_cd140b.description: CD11b+ myeloid cells with PDGFR-beta co-expression, CD206-negative (excludes M2).

myeloid.label: Myeloid Cells
myeloid.description: CD11b+ myeloid cells, all activation and polarization markers negative. Catch-all for myeloid lineage.

activated_immune.label: Activated Immune
activated_immune.description: Non-myeloid leukocytes (CD11b-) co-expressing CD44 and CD140b.

immune_cells.label: Immune Cells
immune_cells.description: CD45+ non-myeloid leukocytes, activation markers negative. Broadest immune catch-all.

activated_endothelial_cd140b.label: Activated Endothelial (CD140b+)
activated_endothelial_cd140b.description: Endothelial cells with PDGFR-beta co-expression, suggesting endothelial-mesenchymal transition.

endothelial.label: Endothelial Cells
endothelial.description: Quiescent endothelial cells (CD31+/CD34+), activation markers negative.

activated_fibroblast_cd140b.label: Activated Fibroblast (CD140b+)
activated_fibroblast_cd140b.description: PDGFR-alpha+ fibroblasts with PDGFR-beta co-expression, suggesting myofibroblast differentiation.

fibroblast.label: Fibroblast
fibroblast.description: PDGFR-alpha+ resident fibroblasts, activation markers negative. Stromal catch-all.

hierarchical_annotation._comment: First-pass broad classification, then refined subtyping

level_2_activation._comment: Classify activation state based on CD44, CD140b expression

biological_analysis._comment: Configuration for downstream biological analysis modules

neighborhood_enrichment._comment: Statistically test which cell types prefer to colocalize

temporal_trajectory._comment: Track how cell type abundances and spatial organization change over time

niche_identification._comment: Identify recurring spatial microenvironments across ROIs and timepoints

cluster_annotation._comment: Map unsupervised clusters to cell types based on marker enrichment
